expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-generator
expectations.evidence: This is a 'without_skill' run. The transcript shows no reading of any SKILL.md file. The agent read package.json, README.md, seed.spec.ts, page.tsx, and layout.tsx only.
No mention of generator_setup_page anywhere in the transcript or output files. The agent never launched a browser; it only read files and wrote the spec.

expectations.text: Executes search/filter steps live in the browser
expectations.evidence: No browser tools (browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_fill_form, etc.) were used. The Tools Used Summary shows only Read (5), Glob (6), Grep (1), Write (3). The agent generated tests purely from code inspection without live browser interaction.

The spec file contains two dedicated no-results tests: 'should show no results message when search yields no matches' (line 79, searches for 'xyznonexistentquery12345' and asserts no-results text is visible and listitem count is 0) and 'should show no results when filter combination yields empty set' (line 102).

expectations.text: Mentions generator_read_log to retrieve execution log
expectations.evidence: No mention of generator_read_log in the transcript or any output file. The agent did not use any generator-specific MCP tools.
No mention of generator_write_test. The agent used the generic Write tool to create search-filter.spec.ts directly, bypassing the skill's dedicated test-writing tool.

expectations.text: Uses resilient selectors (getByRole, getByText, getByLabel) over raw CSS
expectations.evidence: The spec file consistently uses resilient selectors: getByRole('searchbox'), getByRole('combobox'), getByRole('listitem'), getByRole('checkbox'), getByRole('radio'), getByRole('button'), getByPlaceholder(/search/i), getByText(/no results/i), getByText(/nothing found/i). Raw CSS selectors like '[data-testid="search-results"]' appear only as .or() fallbacks, not primary locators.

claims.claim: Tests use resilient locator strategies with .or() chains
claims.evidence: The spec file uses .or() fallback chains extensively, e.g., page.getByRole('searchbox').or(page.getByPlaceholder(/search/i))

claims.claim: The specs/ directory had no specific search/filter test plans
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 2 shows Grep found no files matching search/filter patterns in specs/. The directory only contained README.md.

claims.claim: The Next.js app is a default starter without search/filter features
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 3 confirms only layout.tsx and page.tsx exist, described as 'Default Next.js homepage (no search/filter UI)'
The spec file contains exactly 9 test() blocks within the describe block

claims.claim: Tests were generated based on common search/filter UI patterns
claims.evidence: With no actual search/filter UI in the app and no browser interaction, the tests are indeed generic patterns rather than based on observed behavior. This means they may not match any real implementation.

suggestions.reason: This assertion passes for a spec file that was written speculatively without ever testing against a real app. The tests look correct structurally but were never validated in a browser. Consider requiring that the no-results scenario was actually executed and observed to pass, not just that test code for it exists.

suggestions.assertion: Uses resilient selectors (getByRole, getByText, getByLabel) over raw CSS
suggestions.reason: This assertion is easy to satisfy without the skill — any agent familiar with Playwright best practices will use these selectors. It doesn't discriminate between skill-guided and non-skill runs. Consider checking that selectors were derived from actual page inspection rather than generic patterns.

No assertion checks whether the generated tests actually run successfully against the target application. The agent produced tests for a default Next.js starter that has no search/filter UI, so all 9 tests would fail if executed. An assertion like 'at least one generated test passes when run' would catch this critical gap.

eval_feedback.overall: Five of seven assertions are skill-specific (SKILL.md, generator_setup_page, generator_read_log, generator_write_test, live browser execution) and correctly fail for this without_skill baseline. The two passing assertions (no-results scenario, resilient selectors) are relatively weak — they test code structure rather than functional correctness. The most important missing assertion is whether the generated tests actually work against the target app.
